name: Finland (state flag, 1918–1920)

colors.symbolism: the field; the snows of Finland
the Nordic cross; Finland's lakes and sky
the heraldic lion of the coat of arms
the field of the coat-of-arms shield
the grand duke's crown above the arms

description: A white field with a blue Nordic cross, bearing the Finnish coat of arms — a crowned yellow lion on a red shield — centred on the cross.
history: The first official Finnish state flag, established by the flag act of 29 May 1918 after the blue-cross design was chosen over the rival red-and-gold 'lion flag'. It placed the national coat of arms, surmounted by a grand duke's crown, at the centre of the cross. It was used until 12 February 1920, when revised flag regulations removed the crown from the arms.

features.symbolism: the Nordic cross expressing Finland's place among the Nordic nations

features.role: Finnish coat of arms — a crowned golden lion on a red shield, surmounted by a grand duke's crown
